data.title: Trump Media & Technology Group appoints Kevin McGurn as interim CEO
data.description:
• Trump Media and Technology Group (DJT) on Tuesday announced the appointment of Kevin McGurn as interim chief executive officer, effective immediately.
  • McGurn, who has served as an advisor to Trump Media since December 2024, will succeed current CEO Devin Nunes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: AIOS Tech announces 20-for-1 share consolidation
data.description:
• AIOS Tech (AIOS) announced on Tuesday that its board of directors approved a 20-for-1 share consolidation of its authorized, issued, and outstanding common shares, with the marketplace effective date of April 27, 2026.
  • Immediately upon the effectiveness of the share consolidation, the company will increase its authorized share capital from $100M divided into 480M Class A common shares of a nominal or par value of $0.2 each and 20M Class B common shares of a nominal or par value of $0.2 each to $2B divided into 9.6B Class A common shares of a nominal or par value of $0.2 each and 400M Class B common shares of a nominal or par value of $0.2 each.
  • AIOS Tech said that the objective of the share consolidation is to enable the company to regain compliance with Nasdaq Marketplace Rule 5550(a)(2) and maintain its listing on Nasdaq.
  • Beginning with the opening of trading on April 27, 2026, the company's Class A Common Shares will trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market on a split-adjusted basis, under the same symbol “AIOS” but under a new CUSIP number, G6593L130.
  • As a result of the share consolidation, each 20 common shares outstanding will automatically combine and convert to one issued and outstanding common share without any action for the shareholders. 
  • At the time the share consolidation is effective, the share consolidation shall have the effect of reducing the number of authorized Class A Common Shares from 9.6B Class A Common Shares of a nominal or par value of $0.01 each to 480M Class A Common Shares of a nominal or par value of $0.2 each. 
  • Additionally, the share consolidation shall have the effect of reducing the number of authorized Class B Common Shares from 400M Class B Common Shares of a nominal or par value of $0.01 each to 20M Class B Common Shares of a nominal or par value of $0.2 each, as well as issued and outstanding Class A Common Shares from 64.98M Class A Common Shares of a nominal or par value of $0.01 each to approximately 3.25M Class A Common Shares of a nominal or par value of $0.2 each.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Society Pass receives Nasdaq delinquency notification letter
data.description:
• Society Pass Incorporated (SOPA) on Tuesday said it has received a notice of delinquency letter from Nasdaq as a result of the company’s failure to timely file its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the period ended December 31, 2025.
  • This notice has no immediate effect on the listing of the company’s securities on Nasdaq. 
  • The notice provides that the company has 60 calendar days, or June 15, 2026, to submit to Nasdaq a plan to regain compliance with the Nasdaq listing rule.
  • SOPA -2.03% after hours to $0.5198.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Allied Gaming receives Nasdaq notice over delayed annual report
data.description:
• Allied Gaming & Entertainment (AGAE) on Tuesday said it has received a Nasdaq notice for failing to file its annual report on time.
  • The company said it did not file its Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025 by the required deadline, putting it out of compliance with Nasdaq listing rules.
  • Allied Gaming said the notice has no immediate effect on the listing or trading of its shares.
  • The company has until June 15, 2026 to submit a plan to regain compliance, though it expects to file the report before that deadline.
  • If needed, Nasdaq could grant up to 180 days from the original due date to regain compliance.
  • The company said it is working to complete and file the Form 10-K as soon as possible.
  • AGAE -1.86% after hours to $0.5741.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: FAA sees no current roadblocks to Boeing Max 7, Max 10 approval in 2026
data.description:
The head of the Federal Aviation Administration said Tuesday the agency has not found any problems at this stage that would prevent Boeing Co. (BA) from securing certification for its 737 Max 7 and Max 10 aircraft before the end of 2026.

FAA Administrator Bryan Bedford said in an interview with Bloomberg News that regulators have not seen anything so far suggesting Boeing would miss its year-end certification objective for the two variants.

At the same time, Bedford cautioned that both aircraft remain in flight testing, meaning additional issues could still emerge as the review process continues.

Boeing (BA) Chief Executive Kelly Ortberg said during the company’s January earnings call that the manufacturer was targeting certification of both the smaller Max 7 and larger Max 10 this year. He said Boeing (BA) had been working with regulators on final design updates tied to a safety concern involving the jets’ engine anti-ice system.

Ortberg also said a separate durability matter discovered during inspections of the 777X engine was not expected to interfere with plans to begin deliveries of that long-delayed aircraft in 2027.

Asked about Boeing’s (BA) broader certification timeline, Bedford said he was not aware of any current concerns that would make the company’s targets for the three programs unrealistic.

data.title: Interactive Brokers reports Q1 GAAP EPS below consensus amid lower regulatory fees
data.description:
Interactive Brokers (IBKR) was trading lower as first-quarter earnings fell short of expectations amid a decrease in regulatory fees.

Shares were 2.26% down to $77.82 during post-market trading on Tuesday.

The Greenwich, Connecticut-based company reported adjusted diluted earnings per share of $0.60, higher than $0.47 in the same period a year ago and in line with the average analyst estimate. However, GAAP EPS of $0.59 was below the $0.60 consensus.

Revenue of $1.67B (+16.8% Y/Y) missed the consensus by $10M.

Commission revenue increased 19% to $613M on higher customer trading volumes. Net interest income increased 17% to $904M. Other fees and services increased 10% to $86M. However, execution, clearing, and distribution fees decreased 12% to $106M, driven by lower regulatory fees.

Interactive Brokers raised its quarterly cash dividend to $0.0875 per share from $0.08 per share, payable on June 12 to shareholders of record as of June 1.

data.title: Northpointe Bancshares, Inc. GAAP EPS of $0.62 misses by $0.01, revenue of $63.4M misses by $0.36M
data.description:
• Northpointe Bancshares, Inc. press release (NPB): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.62 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $63.4M (+18.9% Y/Y) misses by $0.36M.

data.title: Weatherford International GAAP EPS of $1.49, revenue of $1.15M
data.description:
• Weatherford International press release (WFRD): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.49.
  • Revenue of $1.15M.
  • First quarter operating income of $123 million decreased 13% year-over-year
  • First quarter net income of $108 million increased 42% year-over-year; net income margin of 9.4%
  • First quarter adjusted EBITDA* of $233 million, decreased 8% year-over-year; adjusted EBITDA margin* of 20.2% decreased 98 basis points year-over-year
  • First quarter cash provided by operating activities of $136 million and adjusted free cash flow* of $85 million
  • Shareholder return of $30 million for the quarter, which included dividend payments of $20 million and share repurchases of $10 million

data.title: Voyager Therapeutics CFO resigns; Robin Swartz takes financial charge
data.description:
• Voyager Therapeutics (VYGR) announced on Tuesday in an SEC Form 8-K filing that its CFO, Nathan Jorgensen, has resigned, effective May 8, 2026, to take up a new opportunity.
  • The Board has appointed Robin Swartz (current COO & CBO) as the new Principal Financial Officer and Treasurer, effective immediately upon transition, ensuring continuity in finance leadership.
  • Adding to the leadership reshuffle, Amy Quinlan has been named Principal Accounting Officer, strengthening the company’s accounting oversight.
  • Following the news, the stock closed ~2.60% lower at ~$4.12.

data.title: Prudential Financial extends its Japan voluntary sales suspension
data.description:
Prudential Financial (PRU)  will extend its voluntary suspension of new sales activity at Prudential of Japan ((POJ)) by an additional 180 days, it said Tuesday, adding that the scope and complexity of the required changes within POJ are greater than previously anticipated.

The extension follows a 90-day suspension that started on Feb. 9, 2026. The company said it will take more time to design and implement operational, governance, organizational, and related changes necessary to resume sales at POJ. The original suspension was conducted to address previously disclosed employee misconduct, including some employees making inappropriate investment solicitations, it said at the time.

Prudential Financial (PRU) also started an independent, third-party review of POJ's management system earlier this year as part of its governance process, which is expected to take several months to complete.

Prudential of Japan remains financially sound and able to meet its obligations, the company said. Japan continues to be a core component of Prudential Financial’s global footprint, and the parent company is providing full support and resources as these reforms advance.

The voluntary suspension does not apply to Prudential Financial’s or PGIM’s other business units in Japan, including Gibraltar Life and Prudential Gibraltar Financial Life.

Prudential Financial (PRU) will hold a conference call  at 5:30 PM ET.
